A year ago, I choreographed this dance. I've been traveling to various live nation amphitheaters to prove that this dance is the perfect dance to dance to any genre of music. Up next, we're going to the Hollywood casino amphitheater in St. Louis to see Odessa. So first I headed over to the green room. They offer complimentary wine, draft beer, and popcorn. I decided to grab myself a liquid death sparkling water, and they had a private air-conditioned restroom, which was really cool. And then I headed over to the lawn terrace, which offered beautiful views of the concert, and I grabbed another liquid death water, and enjoyed the views. Here is some footage of the concert, it was really fun, and I did my signature dance.

This perfume brand is so venereal and slutty that it was locked up in the guerrilla exhibit in the Paxulajican Paris. I'm talking about fatitas, the perfume brand known for its scent inspired by human flesh. Up first is urina bovis, perhaps the most controversial and my guilty pleasure. When wafted this fragrance, Greta Gerwig said she personally regretted not making little women sludger. It has notes of cumin, owl pellet, frog dissection, and leather. This next scent, labia menorah, is like urina bovis's cleaner younger cousin. There's orange marmalade, Bradford pear, freshly inked squid, and fiona apples criminal. This fragrance was created from a sample of the queen's pussy after her tea with Paddington bear. If you have the clean girl aesthetic but wish you could sleep in the locker room, this is the perfume for you.

When I first graduated from college, I was eating out all the time. Partially because of my stressful new job, but also because a man lives in my walls, and he makes brown in my pots and pans, making it really difficult to cook. But now I've made some major changes. I meal prep ahead of time, having access to nutritious foods throughout the workday. Also, I assert a dominance over the man in my walls, and my pots and pans are clean as a whistle. I almost never eat out anymore. Mommy, wow! I'm a big kid now!
